profile.name: Michelle Wong Siew Ling

work_history.title: Front Desk Officer
duties: Managed check-in and check-out for an average of 120–150 guests daily across a 512-room property
Handled multi-nationality VIP guests including Hilton Honors Diamond members with personalised service protocols
Resolved guest complaints related to room type, noise, billing, and service failures — consistently achieving resolution without escalation to duty manager
Operated Opera PMS for reservations management, room assignments, billing, and night audit support
Achieved top upsell revenue in team for 4 consecutive months (Q2–Q3 2024) — RM 28,000 in room upgrades and suite packages
Conducted cross-departmental coordination with housekeeping, concierge, and F&B for seamless guest experience
Assisted in training 2 new front desk colleagues on Opera PMS and Hilton brand standards

work_history.title: Front Desk Associate
duties: Handled check-in, check-out, and room assignment for a 482-room lifestyle hotel property
Managed cash float, foreign currency exchange (USD, SGD, CNY, EUR), and credit card processing
Responded to guest requests via phone, WhatsApp Business, and in-person within agreed SLA
Collaborated with SPG/Marriott loyalty team to process member benefits and point redemptions
Contributed to achieving 4.6/5 average TripAdvisor score for front desk service during tenure

work_history.title: Guest Services Intern
duties: Completed industrial training placement as part of Diploma programme
Rotated through front desk, concierge, and guest relations departments
Observed and assisted with VIP guest arrivals, butler coordination, and MICE event check-ins

certifications: Opera Property Management System (PMS) — Certified User, Oracle Hospitality (2022)
Certified Guest Service Professional (CGSP) — American Hotel & Lodging Educational Institute (2023)
Basic Life Support (BLS) — Malaysian Red Crescent Society (2023, valid 2 years)
Mandarin Chinese for Hospitality Professionals — British Council KL (2022)
profile.parse_notes: All information extracted directly from the CV text. Age calculated based on the date of birth provided.

strengths: The candidate has substantial experience in the hospitality industry, totaling over four years, which aligns well with the outlet supervisor role.
She possesses strong skills in guest relations and complaint handling, which are crucial for managing customer escalations.
Her familiarity with the Opera PMS system and Microsoft Office indicates a solid foundation in using technology for operational tasks.
gaps: Probe in interview for specific experience in team supervision, as her previous roles did not explicitly involve managing a team.
Probe in interview for her experience with cash handling and reconciliation, ensuring she can manage daily cash operations effectively.
score.summary: The candidate is a strong fit for the Outlet Supervisor role with over four years of relevant experience in the hospitality industry and a solid educational background in hotel management. Her skills in guest relations and complaint handling are well-suited for managing customer interactions and escalations. However, further clarification is needed regarding her experience in team supervision and cash handling to ensure she meets all the role's requirements.

strengths: The candidate has substantial experience in the hospitality industry, totaling over four years, which aligns well with the outlet supervisor role.
She possesses strong skills in guest relations and complaint handling, which are crucial for managing customer escalations.
Her familiarity with the Opera PMS system and Microsoft Office indicates a solid foundation in using technology for operational tasks.

profile.name: Nurul Ain Binti Hassan

work_history.title: Barista / Crew Member
duties: Prepared and served beverages according to Starbucks recipes and standards
Handled customer orders at counter and drive-thru with a focus on speed and accuracy
Maintained cleanliness of work station and compliance with food hygiene guidelines
Assisted in closing duties including cleaning, stock checks, and securing the outlet
Received recognition from store manager for positive customer interactions during mystery shopper evaluation (Q3 2024)

work_history.title: Promoter (Contract)
duties: Conducted product demonstrations and sampling for Milo, Nescafé, and Kit Kat product lines
Engaged customers on product benefits and encouraged trial and purchase
Maintained product display stands and ensured adequate stock at promotional booths
Submitted daily sampling and sales reports to event coordinator

work_history.title: Waitress (Part-time)
duties: Took customer orders, served food and managed table turnover during lunch and dinner service
Operated POS system for order entry and payment processing

profile.parse_notes: The CV does not provide specific date of birth or age. Employment dates and durations are calculated based on provided information. Language proficiency levels are inferred from descriptions.

strengths: The candidate has a solid foundation in customer service and cash handling, essential for managing daily operations.
Experience across multiple roles in the food and beverage industry demonstrates adaptability and a broad understanding of the sector.
Proficiency in using POS systems aligns well with the preferred skills for the role.
gaps: Probe in interview for examples of team supervision experience, as this is a key responsibility for the role.
Probe in interview for knowledge and experience with inventory management and staff scheduling.

Their skills in customer service, cash handling, and POS systems are strong, indicating a good fit for the operational aspects of the position. However, further exploration during the interview is needed to assess their experience in team supervision and inventory management, which are critical for the role's leadership responsibilities.
